violations.text: Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Unit taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2024
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024

Raw animal foods (such as chicken) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as cans of soda and vegetables) in the upstairs and basement refrigerators. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024

Open stacked containers of food observed stored directly on top of the food in the containers of food below such as pickles and tomatoes. Food must be protected from contamination by packaging, covered containers, or wrapping. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (cutting board at prep unit, slicer, small blender bowl, rice paddle) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Items are to be cleaned and sanitized before use. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024

Desserts packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024
Food observed stored on the floor beside the grill. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024
Single service items are not being stored properly their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the upstairs area. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024
PIC observed not changing gloves between waiting on customers and preparing food. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the large blender bowl, and the surface beneath grills have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024

The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 04/04/2024
The hand-washing sink in the basement food prep area and toilet room were lacking paper towels. New Violation. Correct By: 04/04/2024
Gyro meat observed stored in the chest freezer. Food must be protected from contamination by packaging, covered containers, or wrapping. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/04/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener, slicer, knives, cleavers) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. All items must be cleaned and sanitized before use. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/04/2024

Buckets of cabbage, beets, and onions observed fermenting at room temperature on the counter in the basement. Disposed. PIC must submit a HACCP plan for fermentation. New Violation. Correct By: 04/04/2024

Thermometers are lacking in the True refrigeration unit. Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 04/05/2024

Pita chips packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. Pita chips were placed behind the service counter. New Violation. Correct By: 04/04/2024
Food observed stored on the floor in the upstairs area, and on the floor in the basement. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 04/04/2024
Utensils (plastic utensils at the prep table) are displayed with the lip surface up. Utensils are to be stored so that only the handles are touched by employees. New Violation. Correct By: 04/04/2024

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the service area, and in basement food prep area. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/04/2024
PIC observed not changing gloves between entering orders at the register and preparing food. New Violation. Correct By: 04/04/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: prep unit surface beneath grills refrigerators freezers Can opener base food storage bins and buckets The chest freezer has an accumulation of frost and debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/04/2024
Water observed on the floor of the basement food prep area due to rain. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/04/2024

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in an undesignated area. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/04/2024

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. (Chicken and lamb kebabs in the same container in prep unit.) Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arrranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/05/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Can opener was taken to 3 bay sink to be thoroughly cleaned and sanitized before use. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/05/2023
Equipment must be cleaned before sanitizing them. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/05/2023

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean on shelves in the basement and service area. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/05/2023
Single service items are being reused for food preparation and storage. Single service items shall not be reused. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/05/2023

Unclean cardboard is used to line the shelf beneath the grills. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. Cardboard must be removed and shelf cleaned. New Violation. Correct By: 05/05/2023

One cutting boards in the basement area is very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 05/12/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris prep unit - both top food area and refrigerator below shelf above prep unit refrigerators refrigerator shelves can opener unused equipment stored in basement counter beneath grill area chest freezer Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/05/2023

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in areas of the establishment. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/05/2023

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in the areas of the establishment. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/05/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the refrigerator shelves, and food prep sink have an accumulation of food residue, and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/05/2023

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the establishment. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2023

The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2023

There was no evidence that employees are required to report to the person in charge information about their health (illnesses, symptoms) as they relate to diseases that are transmissible through food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2023
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2023
PIC observed washing gloved hands. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2023

Foods observed touching areas of the prep unit, and on cardboard which are unclean with assorted food debris. Food must be protected from contamination. All foods are to be removed from prep unit and cooking area, then food prep area cleaned and sanitized before use. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener, bowls, and kitchen ware on the clean storage rack, knives) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. All items are to be cleaned and sanitized before use. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2023
Staff failed to sanitize equipment and utensils after washing and rinsing them. All food contact surfaces must be cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2023

Poultry was cooked to 66 degrees Fahrenheit, then left at room temperature to be cooked per order. Poultry must be cooked to 165 degrees Fahrenheit or above for 15 seconds. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2023
Rice, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Disposed. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2023

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Rice was placed hot in the refrigerator in container greater than four inches deep. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2023

Desserts packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2023
Lettuce observed along the unclean sides of the food prep unit. Food must be protected from contamination. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2023
Food observed stored on the floor in the front area near the hand sink, and the back area. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2023
Wet wiping cloths are not stored in a chemical sanitizer between uses. Wet wiping cloths must be stored in a chemical sanitizer at the appropriate concentration. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2023

Plastic utensils are displayed with the lip surface up. Utensils are to be stored so that only the handles are touched by employees and consumers if consumer self-service is provided. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2023

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the service and basement storage areas. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2023
Single service items observed stored on the floor in the basement storage area. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the prep unit, grill, flattop grill, vertical oven, table beneath vertical oven, shelves, display case, etc. have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2023
The condensate drainage pipe from the ice cream machine is in the hand sink in the front service area. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2023

The hand sink in the front service area has an accumulation of soil residue and food residue.Plumbing fixtures such as handwashing sinks shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2023

The floors throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The condensate pipe from the ice machine in the hand sink has an accumulation of black soil residue. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2023

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement and cooking/front service areas. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2023

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored on single storage containers in the basement. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2023
